eraseUserData.heading: Erase user data
eraseUserData.description: Admin-only, human-verified data erasure. Confirm the requester's identity (forum PM for an unmerged placeholder, matching registered email for a real user) before using this.
eraseUserData.scopeFull: Everything, including the account (case 3 — registered user)
eraseUserData.confirmWarning: This permanently deletes the data shown above. There is no undo.
eraseUserData.networkError: Something went wrong. Check the console and try again.

claim.heading: Claim placeholder account
claim.description: Admin-only, human-verified claim flow. Merges an imported placeholder identity into a real, registered account. Confirm the claimant's identity (forum PM to the site owner's own forum account) before using this — this cannot be undone.
claim.confirmWarning: This merges the placeholder's content into the real account and permanently deletes the placeholder identity. There is no undo.
claim.networkError: Something went wrong. Check the console and try again.

about.heading: About Audiophile Compare
about.whyBody1: Every audiophile knows the feeling: you change a cable, move a speaker, or swap an amp — and it sounds better. But how much of that is the change, and how much is just knowing you made it?
about.whyBody2: Audiophile Compare helps remove that bias. Two clips of the same passage, recorded through your system before and after a change, are labelled only “A” and “B” — which is which stays hidden until you choose to reveal it. Vote on what you actually hear, not what you expect to hear.
about.listenersBody1: Anyone can browse the public feed and listen to the clips — no account needed. Voting, and creating your own tests, needs a free account.
about.listenersBody2: Play clip A and clip B, pick the one you think sounds better for whichever listening technique you're focused on (Tune Method, tonal balance, soundstage, timing, or your own general preference), and optionally note what you heard. You can vote on the same test again using a different technique.
about.listenersBody3: Results stay hidden until you've voted, or the creator reveals which clip was which.
about.creatorsBody1: Describe your system as a “snapshot” — as much or as little detail as you like. Every change becomes a new snapshot, so your system's history is never lost.
about.creatorsBody2: Record two clips of the same passage, before and after your change, and publish them as a blind test. Share the link with anyone you'd like opinions from.
about.creatorsBody3: Reveal the before/after identity whenever you're ready — votes already cast stay exactly as they were.

privacy.heading: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: This page explains what information Audiophile Compare collects, why, and who it's shared with. Audiophile Compare is run by an individual, not a registered company.
privacy.collectBody: If you create an account, I collect your email address. If you register with a password, I store a securely hashed version of it — never the password itself. If you sign in with Google, I receive whatever profile information Google shares (typically your name and email address). I also store whatever you choose to enter using the service: hi-fi systems and their snapshots, tracks, tests, the clip URLs you submit, and your votes, including any optional written observation. Some accounts and content originate from public hi-fi forum posts I've imported rather than from someone registering directly — see "Imported" badges around the site — using a placeholder account until the original poster claims it (see below).
privacy.useBody: This information is used only to run the service — to keep you signed in, to show your own tests and votes back to you, and to display published tests in the public feed. I don't run advertising and I don't use any analytics or tracking tools.
privacy.thirdPartiesBody: Audiophile Compare is built on Supabase, which stores the database and handles sign-in, and is hosted on Vercel. If you choose to sign in with Google, Google acts as your identity provider for that sign-in only — I don't receive any other data from Google. Clips you submit are links to media hosted elsewhere (YouTube, Vimeo, Google Drive, or a direct file URL you provide) — I don't host that media myself, though an automated check periodically confirms a direct link is still reachable.
privacy.cookiesBody: The only cookies this service sets are the ones Supabase Auth uses to keep you signed in. They're essential to the service working and aren't used for advertising or tracking.
privacy.rightsBody: You can update your display name, email, and password from your profile page at any time. There's currently no self-service way to delete your account — if you'd like your account and data removed, email me and I'll action it. If content imported from a public forum post (see above) is actually yours, you can have it attributed to your own account instead of deleted — the same email address will start that process.
privacy.childrenBody: This service isn't directed at children under 16.
privacy.contactBody: This policy is governed by the laws of England and Wales. If you have any questions, contact wpete.callaghan@gmail.com.

terms.heading: Terms of Service
terms.intro: These terms cover use of Audiophile Compare. Audiophile Compare is run by an individual, not a registered company. By using the service you agree to them.
terms.serviceBody: Audiophile Compare lets you run blind A/B listening tests to compare hi-fi systems, components, or recordings, and lets other people vote on what they hear without knowing which clip is which until you choose to reveal it.
terms.accountBody: You're responsible for keeping your account credentials secure and for the accuracy of the information you provide. Please use one account per person — voting is limited to one vote per person per listening technique on each test.
terms.contentBody: You keep ownership of everything you submit — systems, snapshots, tracks, tests, clip links, and votes. Publishing a test makes it visible to anyone in the public feed. You're responsible for making sure any clip URL you submit doesn't infringe anyone's rights — the service only links to media hosted elsewhere and doesn't host or review it.
terms.acceptableUseBody: Don't submit illegal or infringing content, don't create multiple accounts to manipulate vote counts, and don't harass other users.
terms.warrantyBody: The service is provided as-is, with no uptime guarantee. Clip links point to third-party hosted media, which can occasionally become unreachable — this is outside the service's control.
terms.liabilityBody: I'm not liable for decisions you make about hi-fi purchases based on test outcomes, or for the availability of third-party hosted media.
terms.endingBody: You can delete your own tests, snapshots, and systems yourself, as long as nothing else depends on them (a test that's already been voted on, a snapshot still referenced by a test, or a system that still has any snapshot, can't be deleted). Accounts or content may also be removed for abuse of the service.
terms.changesBody: These terms may be updated from time to time; continuing to use the service after a change means you accept the update.
terms.contactBody: These terms are governed by the laws of England and Wales. Questions: wpete.callaghan@gmail.com.
